921 - 13th Avenue 
Delano, CA 
93215 
Executive/leged 
322·5901 
Enforcement 
322-6-Ul 
Re: 
Advice Letter No. A-84-163 
Dear Mr. Hourigan: 
Thank you for your request for advice on behalf of Mr. Van 
Vangel. 
FACTS 
Mr. Vangel is the president of the Board of Directors of the 
North Kern-South Tulare Hospital District. 
Mr. Vangel's wife is 
employed by a doctor as a medical transcriber. 
Recently, the 
Board decided to purchase a CT Scanner, a radiography device, 
which will be leased to a partnership of 25 doctors under a 
lease/purchase agreement. 
The doctor employing Mr. Vangel's 
wife is one of the 25 partners. 
Mr. Vangel abstained from the 
decision on the purchase of the Scanner. 
QUESTION PRESENTED 
How will the Political Reform Act affect Mr. Vangel's future 
decisionmaking regarding the Scanner? 
CONCLUSION 
The Act will require Mr. 'Vangel to disqualify himself on a 
decision concerning the Scanner if it will have a material 
financial effect on the doctor employing Mr. Vangel's wife. 


John T. Hourigan 
July 18, 1984 
Page 2 
DISCUSSION 
Government Code Section 8710011 prohibits a public 
official from making, participating in the making, or in any way 
attempting to use his official position to influence a 
governmental decision in which he knows, or has reason to know, 
he has a financial interest. 
An official has a "financial 
interest" in a decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if 
it is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will have a 
material financial effect,£1 on: 
* * * 
(c) 
Any source of income • • • aggregating two 
hundred fifty dollars ($250) or more in value provided 
to ••• the public. official within 12 months prior to 
the time when the decision is made •••• 
Section 87103. 
The doctor employing Mr. Vangel's wife is a source of 
income to the director. 
(Section 8~030.) 
Mr. Vangel must 
disqualify himself from any decision, including a decision 
concerning the Scanner, if it will have a material 
financial effect on the doctor. 
If Mr. Vangel is unable to 
determine whether to disqualify himself on a particular 
decision, he can contact our office for advice. 
JSM:plh 
Enclosure 
Very truly yours, 
~~~~ 
Janis Shank McLean 
Counsel 
Legal Division 
11 Hereinafter all statutory references are to the 
Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
21 
For an explanatin of the term "material financial 
effect," see the -=!lclosed copy of 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
18702. 


DALE PARHAM 
ASSOCIATE 
June 13, 1984 
Robert E. Leidigh 
Counsel, Legal Division 
Fair Political Practices 
P.O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95814

Van Vangel, president of the Board of Directors of the North Kern -
South Tulare Hospital District, has requested an opL.--ri.on from the 
Fair Political Practices Commission as to his pa.-ticipation as a 
director under the foll~~ circumstances: 
Director Vangel has been on the Board in e..--ccess of 12 years. 
He. ~-ried his present wife 8 years ago. 
She is a forner registered 
nurse and has hourly contract employment as a medical transcriber/ 
secretary T,oJith a general surgeon within t..he :-Iospital Dist:rict area. 
Her time averages 12 to 20 hours per week. and she ea...""nS well in excess 
of the $250. 
She had this job 'tvI1en she married ML. Vangel and he 
was already a director when they married. 
The District does not have a hospital and l1aS accumulated funds 
well in excess of $1,000,000. 
The District proposes to purchase a 
cr Scarmer, a radiography device, and to lease it to a partnership 
fonned and made up for that purpose of some 25 doctors from the sta£f 
of the local conm.mity hospital. 
}bst of the partners are doctors 
and by far the greater percentage are specialists t·mo v7elcorne the 
scarmer in their nractice with the local hospital. 
The District was 
formed at a time ~en the District had little medical and no hosnital 
facilities available and the fortuitious opening of a large private 
hospital in whiCL~ they have taken active interest has provided the only 
hospital/medical attention in North Kern-South Tulare counties. 
A 
large number of its patients are r1ediCal, MediCare and vJelfare. 
The District proposes to purCL~e and ~ake a lease/purchase agree-
fiEI'lt vvith the foregoing partnership. 
The contract ernployer of tb.e 
president IS 'tvife is one of these 25 pB...t..-mers. 
The partnership vlas 
fonned for the sole ~-pose of employkJg b~is scanner for their patients, 
the patients of the local hospital and the entire medical com.mmi.ty. 
TI1e doctors are all lessees and each guarantees payment of the entire 
group as partners. 
Projected earnings apparently v·Jill do little lIDre 
than pay for the scanner, but scanning is now available only from travel-
kl.g units vhlch C0IT'2 periodically from San Francisco and/or Bakersfield. 
The scanner will, in all p::obability, resort to a substantial medical

Re: Conflict of Interest Letter Opinion 
cost to the Delano coom.mity. 
It \\Ould appear ,to be nnre of a 
convenience for the doct~rs. 
A doctor's financial impact on their 
practice is difficult to predict since it is a service that IDUld be 
supplied at any rate to them at a slightly higher cost, which IDUld 
be passed on to the patients. 
Nevertheless, there is a possibility 
of profit to the partners as well as loss, depending upon the use made 
of the sca:rmer. 
The employer/doctor/owner of the sca:rmer will 
apparently have little effect on this overall inCCll::lE. 
This \\Ould not, 
in any way, be reflected on the salary of a ~ca1 transcriber/typist 
(wife of the director). 
The question is as to whether Director Van Vangel can participate in the 
public hearing and voting at the neeting where the lease/purchase contract 
is made. 
Scheduled ~eting date is July 3, 1984 at 8: 00 p. m. 
I appreciate the 
infonnation you gave ~ over the phone this nnming (Tuesday) and IDUld 
appreciate an opinion to this question at your early convenience.

signed and mailed in his 
absence to avoid delay.
